Awesome, you're back. Now I want to thank you for really focusing in that last quiz. I can't even begin to tell you how important it is that you understand the building blocks of NLP anchoring because I use that as the basis for olfactory anchoring. Again, I want to thank Dr. Bambler who really taught me about all things NLP as an awesome dude, an amazing to see in person so if you ever get the chance you should do it. But again, it's one of those cases where I basically stood on the shoulder of a genius and came up with something that I believe is infinitely better than NLP anchoring, which is olfactory anchoring. So let's get right to it, starting with my telling you the story of how I struggled with NLP anchoring. Now the reason I had so much trouble with it with setting an anchor, started by struggle to find a word or a sound or a movement of some sort that felt extreme enough and unique enough to serve as a legitimate anchor that I could fire in any situation. And frankly, once you've tried this for yourself, I think you'll see exactly what I mean. Nothing felt right or profound enough so to speak. So in consequence, when it came time to fire off my anchor to try to get into that peak state of certainty, I never truly felt like I got the benefit. It was almost like I was trying to pull the wool over my own eyes, attempting to convince myself that I'd gotten myself this peak state of absolute certainty when the truth is that when I really have gotten myself into that sense of absolute certainty, there's simply no way I could have missed it. So I struggled with this again and again and again as I kept trying different types of sounds and noises and other potential anchors until I finally hit on the idea of using sense of smell. Now just what made me think of this, I honestly don't recall, but it definitely had something to do with the vivid memories I have from my childhood. You see, to this very day, it never ceases to amaze me how even the slightest whiff of something that I'd smelled in my childhood, like freshly cut fields of grass in the mountains during summer camp, or a low-tie at the jetty where my dad took me fishing with that musky, morph-balled scent of my grandma's house. It was enough to send the most powerful memories literally rushing back to the point where I almost felt that they viscerally hit me. Once I hit on the idea of using sense of smell to set an anchor, it didn't take long to find the perfect product. There were two requirements. First, it had to be a scent that was extreme enough, unusual enough and powerful enough and pungent enough in that matter, to meet Dr. Banner's criteria, yet still be pleasing enough to the nose to not gross me out of a cum its own negative anchor. That was number one. And number two, it had to have an unobtrusive delivery system that was portable and practical and personal to me, meaning I could keep it tucked away in my pocket, remove it without any fanfare and then use it to fire off an anchor without the scent escaping into my surroundings and impacting the people around me. Well, the name of the product I found was called Boom Boom, believe it or not. It's about the size of a chapstick and all I have to do is unscrew the top, give myself a quick blast up each nostril and just like that, boom, boom, I fired off an extremely powerful anchor at a moment's notice and nobody around me is any of the wiser. Makes sense? Now it comes in different flavors. I like winter mint, but the point is that it's strong enough, unique enough and pleasant smelling enough that it instantly fires off my anchor and causes me to pop into a state of absolute certainty. Now, just so you know, I don't own Boom Boom and you can buy it anywhere and you can buy it through me and not me. I don't really care. It's not about that. You can use other things as well. But here's the deal. As amazing as this breakthrough was, it was the breakthrough that it paved the way for that was infinitely more amazing and in fact, what truly turned all factory anchoring into the foolproof state management strategy that it is today. So what was this second incredible breakthrough? Well, simply put, I figured out a laughably simple way to eliminate steps two, three and four from NLP's five step anchoring process. You want to put those up on the screen right now here, please? So by limiting these three steps, I was able to leave behind a leaner, meaner and infinitely more user friendly two step process that I officially dubbed all factory anchoring because of the odorous centerpiece on which its foundation was built, namely Boom Boom. Now to explain exactly how I got to it, let me go back a bit in time. So after I became a master practitioner at NLP at the hands of Dr. Banner, I spent the next six months trying to set an anchor for myself for a state of absolute certainty. No matter how many times I tried, when I got to step four, which is using the five modalities to intensify my focus, things began to break down. The problems started with the memory I chose to focus on. I figured what moment in my life had I ever more absolutely certain than on that magical Tuesday evening when I first drew that straight line on my whiteboard, right? That was my moment of absolute certainty. I couldn't think of a time when I felt more certain than that in my whole entire life, right? But what I discovered here, my own shock was that when I tried to set an anchor using that memory, it just didn't take. There was simply no anchor set. So I tried again and again and again, but no matter how hard I tried, still nothing. So I tried to focus on a different memory. I tried to focus on all memories, new memories, recent memories, memories I was selling, memories where I was training salespeople, memories where I was speaking on stage. I tried everything you could think of, but no matter how powerful the memories were, no matter how many times I tried to use my internal senses to intensify those memories, in my heart of hearts, I knew I hadn't gotten there. I mean, honestly, to truly, truly do this with no bolster or exaggeration is such a major long shot. And most importantly, it's also prone to massive self-delusion where people try to talk themselves into having succeeded because they desperately want the benefit of the anchor. And this is especially common, by the way, in live events where people feel compelled to go along with the crowd. So they jump up and down and they cheer and they clap their hands and scream the word, yes, like wild banshees, and they high-five each other about having just successfully set an anchor. But the stock reality is that the chances of them truly setting an anchor in the midst of all that freaking mayhem were virtually nil. So where am I going with all this? Well, in the end, what I eventually came to realize was that the only surefire away from me to truly be at that ultra-intense level of absolute certainty that's required to set a legitimate anchor was to wait until I was actually there organically and then set my anchor. In other words, why try to manufacture an ultra-peak state of absolute certainty through all these subjective techniques when all I had to do was wait until I closed a really big sale in the real world, in which case I would pop into that state organically and then right at that moment, in that instant when I just closed the sale and was basking in the ethylone feeling absolutely certain, I knew every cell in my body that organically, I was absolutely certain. Really, at the highest level, that's when I would take that one-in-one of boom-boom and bam, just like that. Whoa, I set an anchor that was so powerful that literally it could fire you up in the middle of a freaking hurricane and pop into a state of absolute certainty. Does that make sense? It's so logical. See, guess what? In the real world, it works even better. In other words, you could go out there, right, wait until you're just about to close a sale and as soon as it closes and you're like, hang up the phone or you walk out and you're like, oh man, was I great that day? Yes, then you do it. You take that, yes, while you're in the state organically and then, whoa, you've set yourself a real anchor and here's what you do. After you've done that once, okay, don't use it again, wait until you close another sale and stack another anchor on top of it. So wait until you do your next sale, one-on-one, say yes and squeeze your hands together, right, because everything involved all parts of your body when you're really in the state and guess what? You do that twice, you're done. You've set an anchor using the theories of Bantler and then my adding olfactory anchor on top to set an anchor that is so powerful that I don't care what's going on in your life, that in any moment when it matters most, when you need to be in a state of absolute certainty or membering sales, you're transferring certainty, right? So you have to be in a state of absolute certainty just before you enter the sale, the moment before you either pick up the phone or walk in the door, just go, yes and boom, you are set. You will have an anchor that is so powerful and then all you do from there is use the tactics and strategies of the straight line system to transfer that state of absolute certainty about all three tens, about your product, about yourself, about the company you work for, all three tens at a single moment in time and bam, you can now use the straight line system at the highest possible level and you're pretty much halfway there to being able to close anyone who's closeable.
